entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The ongoing discussions about inflation within the realm of on-chain services highlight a crucial consideration for the potential utility these services could provide in influencing transaction fees. A notable concept that has emerged from these discussions is the implementation of simplistic on-chain "payment codes." Such an approach suggests that with minimal intervention, Bitcoin operators might effectively balance the enhancement of utility against the backdrop of periodic halvings.
Moreover, there appears to be a consensus that the Lightning Network may have absorbed a significant portion of what would have been on-chain fees. This observation underscores the necessity for future protocol developments, specifically those aiming at "compression and privacy" like MWEB (Mimblewimble Extension Blocks), to take into account the dynamics between fee generation and technological enhancements aimed at maintaining user privacy and transaction efficiency. These advancements are essential for sustaining the blockchain ecosystem's growth while addressing scalability and privacy concerns.
